fact_nuggets.text: Intimidate (Japanese: Intimidate) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Lowers the Physical Attack power of attackers. MD T D S Reduces the Physical Attack power of attackers. B S L Conq. Reduces nearby enemies' Attack. MD GtI Reduces damage from physical moves used by attackers next to the Pokemon. * It reduces damage from physical moves of attackers who are next to it! * S MD It reduces damage from physical moves of attackers who are next to it! MD RT DX When the Pokemon is attacked with a physical move by an enemy next to it, the damage is reduced.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation III 1.1.2 Generation IV 1.1.3 Generations V to VII 1.1.4 Generation VIII onward 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation VIII onwards Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokemon with Intimidate enters the battle, it lowers the Attack stat of all adjacent opponents by one stage. In Generations III and IV , Intimidate's activation message reads, " 's Intimidate cuts 's attack!" Intimidate has no effect on Pokemon who are immune to Attack stat reductions, such as Pokemon with Clear Body , Hyper Cutter , or White Smoke . This Ability will not affect a target with a substitute . Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Intimidate now also activates when a Pokemon gains the Ability, such as via Skill Swap or Mega Evolution . Generations V to VII [ edit | edit source ] In Generation V , the message changed to " 's Attack fell!" In a Triple Battle , Intimidate does not affect non-adjacent Pokemon. Intimidate does not affect Pokemon with the Full Metal Body Ability. Wild Pokemon are more likely to call for help when facing a Pokemon with Intimidate. It shares this effect with Pressure and Unnerve . If a Pokemon holding an Adrenaline Orb is affected by Intimidate, the item will be consumed and increase the holder's Speed by one stage. Generation VIII onward [ edit | edit source ] Intimidate no longer affects Pokemon with Oblivious , Own Tempo , Inner Focus , or Scrappy . Intimidate also now triggers Rattled . Intimidate raises the Attack stat of a Pokemon with Guard Dog by one stage, instead of lowering it. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] From Pokemon Emerald onwards, if a Pokemon with Intimidate is in the first place in the party (even if fainted ), there is a 50% chance it will prevent a random wild encounter that would have occurred if the wild Pokemon would be at least 5 levels lower than the Pokemon with Intimidate. It shares this effect with Keen Eye . Generation VIII onwards [ edit | edit source ] Intimidate appears to have no effect outside of battle.

Lowers opponents' Attack one stage upon entering battle.
